Combat sport event in 2023
ONE Fight Night 9: Nong-O vs. Haggerty
The poster for ONE Fight Night 9: Nong-O vs. Haggerty
PromotionONE Championship
DateApril 21, 2023 (2023-04-21)
VenueLumpinee Boxing Stadium
CityBangkok, Thailand
Event chronology
ONE Friday Fights 13: Batman vs. Paidang ONE Fight Night 9: Nong-O vs. Haggerty ONE Friday Fights 14: Gingsanglek vs. Chorfah

ONE Fight Night 9: Nong-O vs. Haggerty was a combat sports event produced by ONE Championship that took place on April 22, 2023, at Lumpinee Boxing Stadium in Bangkok, Thailand.[1][2]

Background

[edit]

A ONE Bantamweight Muay Thai World Championship bout between current champion Nong-O Gaiyanghadao and former ONE Flyweight Muay Thai World Champion Jonathan Haggerty headlined the event.[1]

A strawweight bout between Bokang Masunyane and Hiroba Minowa took place at the event.[3] The pairing was previously scheduled to meet at ONE 159, but the bout was scrapped due to Masunyane missed weight and Minowa rejecting a catchweight bout.[4]

A bantamweight bout between Jhanlo Mark Sangiao and Matias Farinelli took place at the event.[5] The pairing was previously scheduled to meet at ONE 164, but Farinelli has been removed from the card due to tested positive for COVID-19.[6][7]

A bantamweight muay thai bout between Ferrari Fairtex and Felipe Lobo was scheduled for this event.[8] However, due to Ferrari losing to Fabio Reis by knockout at ONE Friday Fights 4, he pulled out from the event and was replaced by Saemapetch Fairtex.[9]

At weigh-ins, Han Zihao weighed in at 149.5 lbs, 4.5 lbs over the Bantamweight limit, and Asa Ten Pow, coming in at 145.25 lbs or .5 lbs over the limit, missed weight and their bout was renegotiated as a catchweight at 149.5 lbs. Matias Farinelli failed his hydration test and was forced to take a catchweight at 149.75 lbs.[10][11][12]

Results

[edit]
Main Card (Amazon Prime Video)
Weight class Method Round Time Notes
Bantamweight Muay Thai Jonathan Haggerty def. Nong-O Gaiyanghadao (c) KO (punches) 1 2:40 [a]
Lightweight Halil Amir def. Maurice Abévi Decision (unanimous) 3 5:00
Strawweight Bokang Masunyane def. Hiroba Minowa Decision (unanimous) 3 5:00
Bantamweight Muay Thai Felipe Lobo def. Saemapetch Fairtex KO (punches) 3 1:56
Women's Atomweight Denice Zamboanga def. Julie Mezabarba Decision (unanimous) 3 5:00
Welterweight Isi Fitikefu def. Valmir da Silva Submission (rear-naked choke) 1 4:15
Women's Strawweight Meng Bo def. Dayane Cardoso Decision (unanimous) 3 5:00
Catchweight (149.5 lbs) Muay Thai Asa Ten Pow def. Han Zihao TKO (punches) 3 0:52
Catchweight (149.25 lbs) Jhanlo Mark Sangiao def. Matias Farinelli Submission (kneebar) 1 0:58
  1. ^ For the ONE Bantamweight Muay Thai World Championship.

[13]

Bonus awards

[edit]

The following fighters were awarded bonuses.[14]

  • Performance of the Night ($100,000): Jonathan Haggerty
  • Performance of the Night: Felipe Lobo, Isi Fitikefu and Jhanlo Mark Sangiao
